subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of how to generate own ideas for artistic expressions on the people based on their history and culture, the environment and topical local/national/global issues. [cite: 2827]
indicator_description: Explore and study the artworks of some international visual artists and analyse how their artworks reflect the history and culture of some communities in the world [cite: 2827]
examplars:
1. use OERs (e.g. library, videos, Facebook, internet) to study and share the artworks of some international visual artists (e.g. Meshack Asare, Zingaro, Leonardo, Michelangelo, Picasso, El-Anatsui, Kara Walker, Ibrahim Mahama, Rebecca Horn); [cite: 2827]
2. discuss the works of the international artists studied in relation to the history and cultures of some communities in the world. For example, Ibrahim Mahama uses the transformation of materials to explore among other themes the issue of migration and the socio-economic and cultural heritage of a people; [cite: 2827]
3. writing a brief about their choice of artist using the following guidelines: Name, Country of origin, Training, Type of artworks, Title of some works, Preferred Media, Contribution to the development of art. [cite: 2827]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of how to generate own ideas for artistic expressions on the people based on their history and culture, the environment and topical local/national/global issues. [cite: 2827]
indicator_description: Study how the artworks of the international visual artists studied reflect the history and culture of some communities in the world and generate own ideas for creating own artworks [cite: 2827]
examplars:
1. select the work of any of the international artists studied; [cite: 2827]
2. reflect on the themes or purposes of the works; [cite: 2827]
3. make an analytical study of artworks to identify the materials, tools, equipment, methods and styles used in designing and making them; [cite: 2827]
4. use the experience gained to generate ideas for own artworks that will reflect the history and culture of some communities in the world. [cite: 2827]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of how to generate own ideas for artistic expressions on the people based on their history and culture, the environment and topical local/national/global issues. [cite: 2830]
indicator_description: Study some artworks created by international visual artists that reflect the physical and social environments of some communities in the world [cite: 2830]
examplars:
1. select the designs and works of any international visual artist that reflect ideas from the physical and social environments of any community in the world; [cite: 2830]
2. appreciate: discuss the selected artworks in groups and share findings with the rest of the class while focusing on the type of artwork, theme/purpose, the idea from the physical and social environment that it reflects, materials and methods/approaches used, etc.; [cite: 2830]
3. select and compare any two of the artworks in terms type, material, method, finishing, function and suitability, etc. [cite: 2830]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of how to generate own ideas for artistic expressions on the people based on their history and culture, the environment and topical local/national/global issues. [cite: 2833]
indicator_description: Generate own ideas for designing and creating own visual artworks based on the physical and social environments of some communities in the world [cite: 2833]
examplars:
1. use OERs to study the physical and social environments of some communities in the world and select objects/ideas that interest them and what they want to do using spidergrams (e.g. Vase, Tray, Pendant); [cite: 2833]
2. present and share selected ideas using the prepared spidergrams; [cite: 2833]
3. reflect and generate ideas from the spidergrams into 'thumbnail' sketches. [cite: 2833]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of how to generate own ideas for artistic expressions on the people based on their history and culture, the environment and topical local/national/global issues. [cite: 2833]
indicator_description: Study the artworks of some international visual artists that reflect topical issues in some communities in the world [cite: 2833]
examplars:
1. select works of international visual artists that reflect emerging topical national issues (e.g. war, racism, terrorism, corruption, rights and responsibilities of road users, counterfeiting, intellectual property etc. digital fraud or 'sakawa'); [cite: 2833]
2. discuss the works in relation to the topical global issues (concepts); [cite: 2833]
3. appreciate: talk about the works based on the type, theme/purpose, technique, style, materials, tools, etc. [cite: 2833]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of how to generate own ideas for artistic expressions on the people based on their history and culture, the environment and topical local/national/global issues. [cite: 2836]
indicator_description: Brainstorm and generate ideas for creating own visual artworks that will reflect topical issues in some communities in the world [cite: 2836]
examplars:
1. use available OERs (e.g. libraries, videos, documentaries, internet, Facebook) to research current topical issues of much concern in some communities in the world; [cite: 2836]
2. document results of findings in the forms of notes, statements, downloads and recordings for presentations and class discussions; [cite: 2836]
3. reflect on feedback from class discussions and peer reviews to generate concepts for designing and making own artworks that will reflect current topical issues of much concern in the world. [cite: 2836]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 2.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of how to generate own ideas for artistic expressions on the people based on their history and culture, the environment and topical local/national/global issues. [cite: 2840]
indicator_description: Explore and study performing artworks of some international performing artists and analyse how their compositions reflect the history and culture of some communities in the world [cite: 2840]
examplars:
1. use OERs (e.g. library, videos, Facebook, internet) to study and share the artworks of some international performing artists (e.g. Michael Jackson, Fred Astaire, Elton John, Bright Okpocha (Basket Mouth), Osei Boateng, Fela Ransome Kuti, Bob Marley, Kojo Antwi, Seidou Keita, Eddie Murphy); [cite: 2840]
2. discuss the works of the international artists studied in relation to the history and cultures of some communities in the world (e.g. 'Exodus' by Bob Marley); [cite: 2841, 2842]
3. write briefs about choice of artists using guidelines: Name, Country of origin, Training, Type of artworks, Title of some works, Preferred compositions and performances, Contribution to the development of the performing arts industry. [cite: 2842]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 2.1.1 Demonstrate understanding of how to generate own ideas for artistic expressions on the people based on their history and culture, the environment and topical local/national/global issues. [cite: 2843]
indicator_description: Study how the artworks of the international performing artists studied reflect the history and culture of some communities in the world and generate own ideas for creating own performing artworks [cite: 2843]
examplars:
1. select the work of any of the international artists studied in B6. 2.1.1.1; [cite: 2844]
2. reflect on the themes or purposes of the works; [cite: 2844]
3. make an analytical study of compositions and performances to identify the musicality, vocabulary, movement patterns, costuming, time, rhythm, speed, energy, space, etc.; [cite: 2845]
4. use the information gained to generate ideas for own artworks that will reflect the history and culture of some communities in the world. [cite: 2846]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 1.2.2 Demonstrate understanding of how to organise own ideas through experimenting with available media and techniques for creating/composing artworks based on the history, culture, environment and topical local/national/global issues of other communities. [cite: 2868]
indicator_description: Experiment with available visual arts media and methods to create visual artworks based on own views, knowledge and understanding of the artworks of some international visual artists [cite: 2868]
examplars:
1. critically examine the works of some international artists; [cite: 2868]
2. make similar works of the international artists studied using available materials and tools from the local environment (e.g. clay, straw, odds and ends, brushes, paint, craft tools, paper folding, needles); [cite: 2869, 2870, 2871, 2872, 2873, 2874]
3. discuss, compare and share their experiences through jury and peer review. [cite: 2874]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 2.2.2 Demonstrate understanding of how to organise own ideas through experimenting with available media and techniques for creating/composing artworks based on the history, culture, environment and topical local/national/global issues of other communities [cite: 2891]
indicator_description: Experiment with available performing arts media and techniques to create performing artworks based on own views, knowledge and understanding of artworks produced by some international performing artists [cite: 2891, 2892]
examplars:
1. critically examine the compositions and performances of some international artists; [cite: 2892]
2. make similar compositions or performances of the international artists studied using available media, techniques, elements, instruments, melodies etc. (e.g. Dance by Michael Jackson, Jokes by Bright Okpocha, Song by Bob Marley, Film by Eddie Murphy); [cite: 2893]
3. discuss and share their experiences through peer review. [cite: 2893]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 1.2.3 Demonstrate understanding of how to create expressive artworks based on own ideas by applying knowledge of media and methods of production to reflect other cultures in Africa, the visual artists, their culture, the environment and emerging topical issues [cite: 2905]
indicator_description: Create functional and decorative visual artworks based on own views, knowledge and understanding of the medium and style of some international visual artists studied [cite: 2906]
examplars:
1. present and share design concepts that reflect knowledge and understanding of the history and culture of some communities in the world; [cite: 2906]
2. demonstrate embedded knowledge, skill and experience in the application and use of selected medium and style (elements/principles, oil paint, clay, cement, carving, printing, weaving, etc.); [cite: 2907, 2908]
3. make own artworks similar to the works and style of the international artists studied using local materials and tools; [cite: 2909]
4. discuss, compare and share experiences through jury and peer review. [cite: 2910]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 2.2.3 Demonstrate understanding of how to create expressive artworks based on own ideas by applying knowledge of media and methods of production to reflect other cultures in Africa, the visual artists, their culture, the environment and emerging topical issues [cite: 2921]
indicator_description: Create own performing artworks based on own views, knowledge and understanding of the techniques and styles of some international performing artists studied [cite: 2921, 2922]
examplars:
1. present and share concepts that reflect knowledge and understanding of the history and culture of some communities in the world; [cite: 2922]
2. demonstrate embedded knowledge, skill and experience in the application and use of techniques and style of an international artist to compose own music, dance, drama, poetry, etc.; [cite: 2923]
3. compose and share own music, dance, drama and poems similar to the works and style of the international artists studied using available media and instruments; [cite: 2924]
4. Discuss compositions through peer review. [cite: 2924]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 1.3.4. Demonstrate understanding of how to plan a display/presentation of a portfolio of own artworks by identifying, and preparing a venue, selecting and grouping of artworks and inviting target audience for the planned display/performance. [cite: 2935]
indicator_description: Plan an exhibition of own functional and decorative visual artworks that reflect the medium and style of some international visual artists studied [cite: 2936]
examplars:
1. watch a short video on an exhibition or visit an exhibition centre; [cite: 2936]
2. discuss the need for displaying portfolio of own visual artworks; [cite: 2937]
3. develop a roadmap (fixing date, selecting venue, inviting audience); [cite: 2938]
4. brainstorm to agree on a theme; [cite: 2938]
5. send manual and/or electronic invitations; [cite: 2939]
6. select works by considering creativity, finishing, relevance to theme; [cite: 2940]
7. decide on mode of display (hanging, draping, spreading); [cite: 2941]
8. plan layout and prepare labels; [cite: 2942]
9. clean and prepare hall; [cite: 2943]
10. plan for post-exhibition activities. [cite: 2944]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 2.3.4 Demonstrate understanding of how to plan a display/presentation of a portfolio of own artworks by identifying, and preparing a venue, selecting and grouping of artworks and inviting target audience for the planned display/performance. [cite: 2966]
indicator_description: Plan a display of own performing artworks to share creative experiences of the techniques and styles of some international performing artists studied [cite: 2967]
examplars:
1. watch a short video or live performances that reflect emerging topical issues; [cite: 2967]
2. discuss the need for performing compositions; [cite: 2968]
3. develop a roadmap (fixing date, selecting venue, inviting audience); [cite: 2969]
4. brainstorm theme; [cite: 2969]
5. send invitations (manual/electronic); [cite: 2970]
6. select compositions based on creativity, lyrics, dynamics, and relevance; [cite: 2971]
7. decide on mode and arrangement (solo/group, costumes, props); [cite: 2972]
8. plan sequence of events and site plan (PA, lights, changing rooms); [cite: 2973]
9. clean and prepare venue; [cite: 2974]
10. plan for post-performance activities (cleaning, appraisal, reporting). [cite: 2975]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 1.3.5 Demonstrate understanding of how to display/present a portfolio of art works that share own knowledge, concepts, ideas and experiences with audience through display/presentation; and using senses/manual/digital applications where necessary, to record for reporting on the events. [cite: 3010, 3011]
indicator_description: Exhibit own visual artworks to share creative experiences based on ideas, knowledge and understanding of the medium and style of some international artists studied [cite: 3012]
examplars:
1. decide on the types and number of artworks to exhibit and mount based on available space and theme; [cite: 3012]
2. display artworks by hanging, draping, placing, spreading, leaning, using plinths; [cite: 3013]
3. label the works (artist, title, size, date); [cite: 3014]
4. assign tasks and responsibilities (ushering, explaining concepts, recording comments); [cite: 3015]
5. check/monitor visitors to prevent improper practices; [cite: 3016]
6. organise opening and closing ceremonies. [cite: 3017]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 2.3.5 Demonstrate understanding of how to display/present a portfolio of art works that share own knowledge, concepts, ideas and experiences with audience through display/presentation; and using senses/manual/digital applications where necessary, to record for reporting on the events. [cite: 3034, 3035]
indicator_description: Stage a display of own performing artworks to share creative experiences of the techniques and style of international performing artists studied [cite: 3036]
examplars:
1. decide on types and number of compositions based on theme, time, and audience; [cite: 3036]
2. assign individual and group tasks (MC, ushering, explaining titles/theme); [cite: 3037, 3038]
3. arrange sequence of performances to tell the story from beginning to end; [cite: 3039]
4. follow programme of the day (opening, performances, closing); [cite: 3040]
5. prepare template for appraisal and report; clean hall after. [cite: 3041]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 1.4.6 Demonstrate understanding of how to analyse, appreciate, appraise/critique and present report on own works and that of others based on established guide for judging artworks (the theme, subject matter, media, techniques, elements and principles of design, social, aesthetic, cultural and functional values), the recordings and reports [cite: 3057]
indicator_description: Develop guidelines for appreciating and appraising own and others’ visual artworks that reflect the medium and style of the international visual artists studied [cite: 3057, 3058]
examplars:
1. discuss and accept a guide based on elements, materials, subject matter, appraisal, and experiences; [cite: 3058, 3062, 3063]
2. identify correct vocabulary; [cite: 3059]
3. agree on report usage and approach (manual/digital); [cite: 3060, 3061]
4. fix date for jury. [cite: 3062]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 2.4.6 Demonstrate understanding of how to analyse, appreciate, appraise/critique and present report on own works and that of others based on established guide for judging artworks (the theme, subject matter, media, techniques, elements and principles of design, social, aesthetic, cultural and functional values), the recordings and reports [cite: 3077]
indicator_description: Develop guidelines for analysing and appreciating own and others’ performing artworks that reflect the technique and style of international performing artists studied [cite: 3077, 3078]
examplars:
1. discuss and accept a guide (Music: theme/diction/harmony; Dance: movements/energy/stage; Drama: characterisation/gestures/use of space); [cite: 3078, 3082]
2. identify correct vocabulary; [cite: 3079]
3. agree on report usage and documentation approach; [cite: 3080, 3081]
4. fix date for jury. [cite: 3082]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 1.4.7 Demonstrate understanding of how to make informed decisions on displays, presentations, performances, recordings and/or reports based on established guide for judging artworks (the theme, subject matter, media, techniques, elements and principles of design, social, aesthetic, cultural and functional values) for correlation, correction and future modifications [cite: 3097, 3098]
indicator_description: Analyse and appreciate own or others’ artworks and present reports as feedback on artworks that reflect the medium and style of international performing artists studied [cite: 3098]
examplars:
1. display selected works (real/photo/video); [cite: 3098]
2. talk about works dispassionately using agreed guidelines; [cite: 3099]
3. use outcome to modify product or produce similar/other work; [cite: 3100]
4. record and share using social media (Facebook, Twitter, etc.). [cite: 3101, 3102]

subject: Creative Arts
content_standard: B6 2.4.7 Demonstrate understanding of how to make informed decisions on displays, presentations, performances, recordings and/or reports based on established guide for judging artworks (the theme, subject matter, media, techniques, elements and principles of design, social, aesthetic, cultural and functional values) for correlation, correction and future modifications [cite: 3113]
indicator_description: Analyse and appreciate own or others’ performing artworks and present reports as feedback on artworks that reflect the techniques and styles of the international performing artists studied [cite: 3113, 3114]
examplars:
1. display selected performances (photo/video); [cite: 3114]
2. talk about performances dispassionately using guidelines; [cite: 3115]
3. use outcome to modify composition/performance; [cite: 3116]
4. record and share using social media. [cite: 3117]
